additionalFeedback: Couldn't find any other players I guess

keyFactors: Ratio of success to failure and total overall successes.
strategy: I selected the candidates with both the highest successes and the highest success to failure ratios. I then occasionally switched it up if a candidate had a better ratio of success to failure even if they had fewer overall successes.
additionalFeedback: It would have been more interesting to get information at the end about the other people's choices. I thought I was working as a team but it didn't feel like it, what little information I was given was hard to know what anything meant.

strategy: Got sent to the exit survey again

keyFactors: I prioritized groups with higher success-to-failure ratios, as these indicated consistent and reliable performance.
strategy: I looked at the ratio of successes to total attempts (successes + failures) for each group to assess their overall productivity and reliability.
motivation: I was motivated by the challenge of identifying the most productive groups to maximize success and contribute to the overall goal of building a high-performing team.

strategy: I picked the one who had the most tries.
motivation: It looked interesting and paid well for my time.

keyFactors: Experience but also consistency. It doesn't matter if you can produce a lot if what you are doing is wrong and failing.
strategy: I weighed productivity based on the success to failure ratio. Even if an applicant had a higher number of successes than another applicant if they had more failures that factored in their ratio, I would pick the applicant with the smaller number of successes.
It was based on success.  The failures were still within range of a highly success employee.

keyFactors: The numbers, and the percentages for success for each group.
strategy: At first, I wasn't certain how to approach this game and ultimately decided to use percentages to try and calculate the best opportunities for success.
motivation: This survey caught my eye because I usually enjoy the challenge of playing brain games. I believe it's good for all of us to engage in such activities, no matter what age we are. The payment is also much appreciated.

keyFactors: I felt the higher the success, the better - even if there was a high failure rate. We were asked to select the most productive and I would think the amount shows productivity, whether failing or succeeding.
strategy: I mainly looked at success before failure numbers and then went from there.
motivation: The payout for 30 mins was a good motivation and it seemed like it would be engaging.
